claim: Brussels Airport (BRU) will offer direct flights to 180 destinations in 2026 with 70 airlines, season starting March 29.
quote: Brussels Airport will offer direct flights to 180 destinations this summer, with 70 airlines operating during the 2026 season starting March 29.

claim: Ryanair operates 10 weekly flights from Charleroi (CRL) to Tenerife South (TFS) with average flight time 4h30, sole operator on the route.
quote: There are 10 flights per week flying from Brussels Charleroi to Tenerife (Reina Sofía) (as of January 2026). The average flight time is 4 hours and 30 minutes.

claim: Charleroi (CRL) has Ryanair direct service to Tenerife, Gran Canaria, Lanzarote, Fuerteventura, Madeira, Faro, Paphos, Heraklion, Chania, Rhodes, Athens, Catania, Palermo, Málaga and Sevilla; Athens also served by Pegasus.
quote: non-stop passenger flights scheduled to 129 destinations in 30 countries

claim: Aegean is the only carrier flying nonstop from Brussels (BRU) to Larnaca (LCA), with 5 weekly flights as of January 2026 and a flight time of around 4 hours.
quote: there are 5 flights per week flying from Brussels to Larnaca (as of January 2026)... only one airline operating non-stop flights from Brussels BRU to Larnaca LCA, which is Aegean.

claim: Brussels Airlines operates direct flights from BRU to Heraklion (HER), Crete.
quote: Flight from Brussels (BRU) to Heraklion - Crete (HER) | Brussels Airlines

claim: Brussels Airlines operates 8 weekly nonstop flights from BRU to Heraklion with average flight time 3h20 (April 2026).
quote: Brussels Airlines operates nonstop flights from Brussels to Heraklion (Crete), with 8 flights per week as of April 2026. The average flight time is 3 hours and 20 minutes.

claim: BRU to Funchal (Madeira) has 3 weekly nonstop flights with average duration 4h30; Brussels Airlines and TUI Fly are the operators.
quote: There are 3 flights per week flying from Brussels to Funchal (Madeira) as of April 2026. Brussels Airlines and TUI Fly are flying nonstop from Brussels to Funchal (Madeira). The average flight time from Brussels to Funchal (Madeira) is 4 hours and 30 minutes.

claim: Brussels Airlines and TUI Fly fly direct from BRU to Catania (CTA), seasonal April–November.
quote: You can fly non-stop to Catania with Brussels Airlines (Star Alliance) or TUI Fly, as a seasonal route that starts in April and ends in November.

claim: TUI fly Belgium operates 2 weekly nonstop flights from BRU to Paphos (PFO) with flight time 4h25; Ryanair also serves the route.
quote: There are 2 direct flights between Brussels and Paphos every week, operated by TUI fly Belgium with a flight time of 4 hours and 25 minutes. Additionally, Ryanair connects Brussels and Paphos with direct flights to Paphos airport.

claim: Ryanair is the sole nonstop carrier from Charleroi (CRL) to Malta (MLA), operating 9 weekly flights with departures spread 07:05–21:50.
quote: Ryanair is the only airline flying nonstop from Brussels Charleroi to Malta. Flights from CRL to Malta are operated 9 times a week, with an average of 1 flight per day, and departure times vary between 07:05 - 21:50.

claim: Ryanair flies CRL to Málaga (AGP) 11 times weekly (about 2 daily), sole operator on the route.
quote: Flights from CRL to Málaga are operated 11 times a week, with an average of 2 flights per day, and departure times vary between 06:40 - 22:00. Ryanair is the only airline flying nonstop from Brussels Charleroi to Málaga.

claim: CRL to Faro (FAO) drops to 1 weekly Ryanair flight by March 2026 (off-season), sole operator.
quote: There is 1 flight per week flying from Brussels Charleroi to Faro (as of March 2026). Ryanair is the only airline operating non-stop flights from Brussels Charleroi CRL to Faro FAO.

claim: BRU to Tenerife South (TFS) has 11 weekly nonstop flights with average flight time 5 hours; Brussels Airlines, Transavia and TUI Fly all operate the route.
quote: There are 11 flights per week flying from Brussels to Tenerife (Reina Sofía) (as of April 2026), with an average flight time of 5 hours. Brussels Airlines, Transavia and TUI Fly are flying nonstop from Brussels to Tenerife.

claim: BRU–Athens has ~25–26 weekly direct flights operated by Brussels Airlines, Aegean and SAS, with flight time approximately 3h20.
quote: There are 25 flights per week flying from Athens to Brussels (as of April 2026)... Brussels Airlines, Aegean Airlines and Scandinavian Airlines fly direct from Athens International to Brussels.

claim: Brussels Airlines runs 9 weekly nonstop flights BRU–Málaga (AGP) with flight duration 2h55; Vueling also serves the route.
quote: Brussels Airlines is the most popular for nonstop flights on this route, offering 9 nonstop flights per week from Brussels Airport (BRU) to Málaga-Costa del Sol Airport (AGP)... flight duration of 02:55 hours.

claim: Brussels Airlines flies BRU–Faro (FAO) with 6 weekly nonstop services.
quote: Brussels Airlines is the most popular for nonstop flights, offering 6 nonstop flights per week from Brussels Airport (BRU) to Faro International Airport (FAO).

claim: Brussels Airlines operates ~2 weekly direct flights from BRU to Chania (CHQ) with flight time 3h25.
quote: Brussels Airlines operates approximately 2 weekly flights from Brussels to Chania with a flight duration of 03:25 hours.

claim: Brussels Airlines flies from BRU direct to Athens, Heraklion, Chania, Kos and Rhodes.
quote: Flights to Greece | Brussels Airlines

claim: Ryanair operates the CRL–Malta route with flight time 2h55.
quote: The route between Malta and Brussels South Charleroi Airport is operated by Ryanair with a flight time of 2 hours and 55 minutes.

claim: Brussels Airlines confirms a direct service from BRU to Málaga.
quote: Flight from Brussels (BRU) to Málaga (AGP) | Brussels Airlines

claim: Vueling operates direct flights from Brussels to Málaga.
quote: Cheap flights Brussels to Malaga | Vueling

claim: Transavia operates ~4 weekly nonstop flights BRU to Seville (SVQ); Brussels Airlines does not serve this route directly.
quote: Transavia is the most popular airline for nonstop flights to Seville, offering 4 nonstop flights per week from Brussels Airport (BRU) to Seville Airport (SVQ).
